title: evia dulcis
intro: Evia dulcis, also known as wild licorice, is a plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible root system. The root of Evia dulcis contains glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and medicinal properties, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and providing antioxidant properties to protect against cell damage. Evia dulcis is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
